column4: Description

Soviet Issued Medical Aid Box, offers the bare necessities for healing and treating wounds.
Extremely light, and looks a bit like a craft single.

Commercial Brand Gauze for dressing wounds.
Not entirely strong in the field, but it does it's job.

Medication used to mediate severe pain. 
Acts directly towards the central nervous system. 
Like Painkillers, except extremely effective and dangerous when misused.

Cheap and Widely Spread Painkillers.
Effects don't linger for long, but it is cheap. [Four Uses, each use takes two pills.]
Common splint for fixing broken bones into place.
Stops the flow of blood to prevent bloodloss. Reliable but slow to apply.

Medical Kit specializing in removing radiation and or poison accumulated in the body. 
Can be used to treat Chemical Burns. [Regens after Deployment]

Packing Gauze specifically made to treat combat wounds when medical personnel are not available.
Use in conjunction with other bandages to effectively stabilize gunshot wounds.

Basic Field Surgery Kit.
Provides the means to preform surgery on your arms and legs, but not much else. [Four Uses]

Military-Issued Gauze Bandage for dressing wounds.
Due to the nature of it's design, it's effects are nearly immediate compared to commercial brands.
Bare-bones First Aid Kit. Can only treat minor injuries.
General Use First Aid Kit, able to treat standard injuries and lacerations.

Advanced Field Surgery Kit.
Able to heal severe injuries on the field, but takes quite a while. [Five Uses]

For usage alongside the Surv12 Field Kit. 
Enables user to transfuse artifcial blood alongside healing injuries for maximum, temporary recovery.
Fast Acting Tourniquet. Extremely straight forward, and is often time faster than a normal Esmarch. [Re-usable.]

State of the art Anti-Biotic utilized to treat a large array of bacterial infections, and to numb pain for extended periods of time
[15 Uses, Single Pill.]

Military Issue First Aid Kit used before proper medical attention can be applied.
Able to mediate severe injuries on-top of treating standard injuries and lacerations. [Can be Refilled for Half the Price.]

Hemostatic Injector that prevents the applied wound from bleeding further. Extremely Useful in combat intense situations.
{Does not take up an action during combat.]
Description

Standard Issue MRE, given to those over prolonged periods of fighting. 
It's engineered to be good for you, but some like to disagree.

Protein Drink flavored in an assortment of flavors
Not really noticable, but it tastes like you're drinking thick sludge half the time.

Fresh and Purified Water using a CR-100 Water Filter. 
Holds about 0.6L of Water. Internally Chilled.

A Five Pack Assortment of various noodles ranging from Ramen to Yakisoba.
Can be eaten as is or heated up properly as instructions stated.

A Field Kitchen Kit consisting of metallic canteen cups, utensils and dry fuel.
Relatively Heavy coming in at a total of 5 kgs.

Camping Set consisting of a setup tent and sleeping bag.
Heavy, and often times used in operations where company support is sparse.
Simple Firestarter set consisting of various dry fuels, tinder and lighters.

Portable Battery Charge for use in on-field battery charging.
It is solar-powered, and as such does not require manual charging. (The option however is still available.)
Description
Medical Adrenaline. Commerically available under the right circumstances, it's often used to enhance one's senses.
Medical Injector for Regulating Heart Rate. Not particularly useful for combat purposes, but is handy for ensuring a patient's recovery after extensive on-field medical procedures.
